nodes.label: Isaac GR00T N1.7 Project Overview
nodes.rationale: AGENTS.md describes the repo as containing the model, training pipeline, evaluation harness, and deployment tooling.

nodes.label: Third-Party License Attributions
nodes.rationale: ATTRIBUTIONS.md explicitly inventories dependency names, versions, homepages, copyrights, and license texts.

nodes.label: Quick-Start Development Commands
nodes.rationale: CLAUDE.md lists uv install, pre-commit, pytest, uv build, and uv lock commands for this repo.

nodes.label: Early Access Contribution Policy
nodes.rationale: CONTRIBUTING.md states pull requests are not accepted during Early Access while the codebase stabilizes.

nodes.label: GR00T Data Strategy Guidance
nodes.rationale: FAQ.md gives post-training data volume guidance, recommends HG-DAgger, and discusses cross-embodiment data benefits.

nodes.label: Policy Remote Server
nodes.rationale: FAQ.md says the Policy Remote Server decouples inference from the physical robot and lets inference run on high-compute clusters.

nodes.label: NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.7
nodes.rationale: README.md defines GR00T N1.7 as an open VLA model for generalized humanoid robot skills.

nodes.label: VLM plus Diffusion Transformer Architecture
nodes.rationale: README.md states the architecture combines a vision-language foundation model with a diffusion transformer head for continuous actions.

nodes.label: Relative End-Effector Action Space
nodes.rationale: README.md identifies relative EEF actions as a key N1.7 change that improves cross-embodiment generalization.

nodes.label: EgoScale Human Video Pretraining
nodes.rationale: README.md states N1.7 uses 20K hours of EgoScale human video data in pretraining.

nodes.label: Hugging Face GR00T N1.7 Checkpoints
nodes.rationale: README.md lists base and finetuned N1.7 checkpoints, including DROID, LIBERO, and SimplerEnv variants.

nodes.label: Embodiment Tags
nodes.rationale: README.md says every inference or finetuning command requires an embodiment tag to choose modality config and normalization.

nodes.label: GR00T LeRobot Dataset Format
nodes.rationale: README.md describes a LeRobot v2 dataset flavor with GR00T-specific meta/modality.json metadata.

nodes.label: Containerized GR00T Environment
nodes.rationale: docker/README.md explains how to build and run GR00T containers for x86_64, aarch64, Thor, Spark, and Orin.

nodes.label: DROID Dataset and Embodiment
nodes.rationale: DROID README documents DROID modality structure, the OXE_DROID_RELATIVE_EEF_RELATIVE_JOINT tag, sample data, inference, fine-tuning, and robot control.

nodes.label: LIBERO Benchmark
nodes.rationale: LIBERO README describes Spatial, Object, Goal, and 10 Long suites for lifelong robot learning and GR00T evaluation.

nodes.label: SO100 Custom Embodiment Workflow
nodes.rationale: SO100 README shows LeRobot conversion, NEW_EMBODIMENT fine-tuning, open-loop evaluation, and real-robot Policy API deployment.

nodes.label: SimplerEnv Benchmark
nodes.rationale: SimplerEnv README covers Google Robot and WidowX/Bridge fine-tuning and server-client simulation evaluation.

nodes.label: Mask-Guided Background Suppression
nodes.rationale: The mask-guided README defines per-frame segmentation mask augmentations, including background noise and masked color transforms.

nodes.label: ModalityConfig
nodes.rationale: data_config.md defines ModalityConfig for video, state, action, and language loading, temporal sampling, and modality keys.

nodes.label: ActionConfig
nodes.rationale: data_config.md defines ActionConfig fields for relative or absolute actions, EEF or non-EEF type, representation format, and optional state_key.

nodes.label: meta/modality.json
nodes.rationale: data_preparation.md requires meta/modality.json to split concatenated state/action arrays and map video and annotation fields.

nodes.label: LeRobot v2 Data Preparation
nodes.rationale: data_preparation.md says GR00T currently uses LeRobot v2 and instructs users to convert v3 or other formats into compatible v2 layout.

nodes.label: NEW_EMBODIMENT Fine-Tuning
nodes.rationale: finetune_new_embodiment.md demonstrates preparing data, registering modality config, fine-tuning, and open-loop evaluation for custom robots.

nodes.label: GR00T Hardware Profiles
nodes.rationale: hardware_recommendation.md separates inference/deployment and fine-tuning profiles with VRAM, platform, and TensorRT performance guidance.

nodes.label: GR00T Policy API
nodes.rationale: policy.md explains Gr00tPolicy loading, observation/action formats, get_action, modality config queries, batching, and action chunking.

nodes.label: ZeroMQ Policy Server-Client Architecture
nodes.rationale: policy.md documents PolicyClient and PolicyServer using ZeroMQ REQ/REP and msgpack endpoints for remote inference.

nodes.label: ReplayPolicy
nodes.rationale: policy.md describes ReplayPolicy as a way to replay recorded dataset actions for debugging environment integration and server-client communication.

nodes.label: Real-World VLA Deployment Workflow
nodes.rationale: real_world_deployment.md outlines hardware preparation, data collection, preprocessing, training, deployment, and closed-loop testing.

nodes.label: Real-Time Chunking
nodes.rationale: real_world_deployment.md explains RTC as overlapping action predictions with asynchronous inference to reduce stop-and-go behavior.

nodes.label: TensorRT Full Pipeline
nodes.rationale: scripts/deployment/README.md defines trt_full_pipeline and build_trt_pipeline.py for ONNX export, engine build, verification, and benchmarking.

nodes.label: Deployment Platform Matrix
nodes.rationale: scripts/deployment/README.md gives platform-specific setup for dGPU, Jetson Thor, DGX Spark, and Jetson Orin, including CUDA/Python differences.

nodes.label: convert_v3_to_v2.py
nodes.rationale: scripts/lerobot_conversion/README.md documents using convert_v3_to_v2.py inside a uv environment.

nodes.label: CI Test Fixtures
nodes.rationale: tests/fixtures/README.md defines small static fixtures that let CI tests run without checkpoints, datasets, network access, or large binaries.
